<!-- cursoride Session d6ceb2b1-4ede-4072-bcc5-970e42d0d0b7 (2026-04-14 06:42:56Z) -->

_**User**_

Here is base url  Base URL: https://api.themoviedb.org/3

All requests must include Authorization: Bearer {TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN} set by the central Axios interceptor — not manually on individual calls.

Below are the Endpoints which we need to write in existing @streamlist/src/api/movies.ts 
Endpoint	                                                	Key Response Fields
GET /trending/movie/week - 	results[].id, title, poster_path, backdrop_path, vote_average, release_date, genre_ids
GET /movie/top_rated - 	          results[].id, title, poster_path, backdrop_path, vote_average, release_date, genre_ids
GET /genre/movie/list -                                        genres[].id, genres[].name
GET /discover/movie?with_genres={id}          	results[].id, title, poster_path, backdrop_path, vote_average, release_date, genre_ids

GET /search/movie?query={q}	- 	    results[].id, title, poster_path, vote_average, release_date, genre_ids
GET /movie/{id}                            -                     backdrop_path, vote_average, release_date, genres[], runtime, overview
GET /movie/{id}/credits	-                        	cast[].name, cast[].character, cast[].profile_path
GET /movie/{id}/similar	-                           	results[].id, title, poster_path

Image URLs
TMDB returns image paths, not full URLs. Construct them using:

https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/{size}{path}
Size	Use Case
w185	Cast avatars, small thumbnails
w342	Standard portrait content cards
w780	Detail screen backdrop
Use @streamlist/src/utils/image.ts file here

Pagination
Paginated endpoints accept a page query param (default: 1, max varies). Each page returns up to 20 results. total_pages in the response indicates how many pages exist. Request the next page when the user scrolls within 3 items of the row end.

Do not map with any screen for now. we will do that later



---

I'm generating the API files for TMDB movies feature, starting with env types and client implementation. I'm implementing the SimilarMovieItem type with id, title, and poster_path fields based on TMDB response structure. </details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was implemented.

### Auth (`src/api/client.ts`)
- Replaced query `api_key` with **`Authorization: Bearer ${TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN}`** via `config.headers.set(...)` on the shared Axios instance.

### Env
- **`types/env.d.ts`**, **`__mocks__/env.js`**, **`.env.example`**: `TMDB_API_KEY` → **`TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN`** (empty placeholder in `.env.example`; comment explains using the TMDB “API Read Access Token”).
- **Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and set `TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN`** (rename from the old variable if you still have a local `.env`).

### Types (`src/api/types.ts`)
- Added **`MovieListItem`**, **`Genre`**, **`GenreListResponse`**, **`MovieDetails`**, **`CastMember`**, **`MovieCredits`**, **`SimilarMovieItem`**, and kept **`PaginatedResponse`** / **`MovieSummary`**.

### API (`src/api/movies.ts`)
| Function | Endpoint |
|----------|----------|
| `fetchTrendingMoviesWeek(page?)` | `GET /trending/movie/week` |
| `fetchTopRatedMovies(page?)` | `GET /movie/top_rated` |
| `fetchMovieGenres()` | `GET /genre/movie/list` |
| `fetchDiscoverMoviesByGenre(genreId, page?)` | `GET /discover/movie` + `with_genres` |
| `searchMovies(query, page?)` | `GET /search/movie` |
| `fetchMovieDetails(movieId)` | `GET /movie/{id}` |
| `fetchMovieCredits(movieId)` | `GET /movie/{id}/credits` |
| `fetchSimilarMovies(movieId, page?)` | `GET /movie/{id}/similar` |

All use **`apiClient`** only; pagination uses optional **`page`** (default `1`).

### Images (`src/utils/image.ts`)
- Exported **`IMAGE_SIZE_CAST`** (`w185`), **`IMAGE_SIZE_CARD`** (`w342`), **`IMAGE_SIZE_BACKDROP`** (`w780`).
- Default size for **`buildImageUrl`** is now **`w342`** (cards).

### Tests
- **`__tests__/movies.test.ts`** – asserts paths and params for each function (8 tests, passing).
- **`npm test`** still fails on **`App.test.tsx`** because of **`react-native-vector-icons`** ESM in Jest (existing issue, not from this change).

**Note:** Creating git branch failed here (sandbox permissions). You can run locally:  
`git checkout -b cursor/tmdb-movies-api` before committing.

please confirm if this is done  - All requests must include Authorization: Bearer {TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN} set by the central Axios interceptor — not manually on individual calls.

**Yes — that requirement is satisfied.**

The **only** place `Authorization: Bearer …` is set is the **request interceptor** on the shared `apiClient` in `client.ts`:

```9:14:/home/tusharsaini/CursorAI-training/Phase 2/streamlist/src/api/client.ts
apiClient.interceptors.request.use((config: InternalAxiosRequestConfig) => {
  if (TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN.length > 0) {
    config.headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN}`);
  }
  return config;
});
```

`movies.ts` uses `apiClient.get(...)` with **paths and `params` only** — no `headers`, no `Authorization`, and no per-call token logic. For example:

```16:18:/home/tusharsaini/CursorAI-training/Phase 2/streamlist/src/api/movies.ts
  const response = await apiClient.get<PaginatedResponse<MovieListItem>>('/trending/movie/week', {
    params: { page },
  });
```

**Practical note:** The header is applied when `TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN` is non-empty in `.env`. If it’s empty, requests go out **without** `Authorization` (TMDB will reject them until the token is set).
